description: Dereferencing or freeing a pointer after the memory it points to has already been freed.
body: Failure mode: code keeps using a pointer after free() (or frees it twice), reading or writing reclaimed memory that may have been reallocated, causing corruption or crashes that depend on allocator state. Detection signals: ASan reports heap-use-after-free with the alloc/free stacks; intermittent crashes far from the free; Valgrind 'Invalid read/write'. Do not retain a pointer past the free that owns it. Safer fallback: set the pointer to NULL immediately after free, establish single clear ownership, and re-run under ASan/Valgrind to confirm the use-after-free is gone.
